summary:
The most common symptom of aortoiliac occlusive disease is calf claudication due to muscle ischemia caused by reduced arterial flow. This condition manifests primarily through intermittent leg pain triggered by activity but relieved at rest, distinguishing itself from other potential manifestations associated with milder forms of the disease. ```plaintext
The most common symptom of aortoiliac occlusive disease is calf claudication,表现为间歇性腿部疼痛，活动时出现但休息后缓解。这种症状主要由动脉供血不足导致的肌肉缺氧引起，并且区别于其他较轻形式疾病可能伴随的症状。
``` Summary:

Calf claudication is the most frequently observed symptom of aortoiliac occlusive disease, manifesting as painful cramping in the legs during physical activity that resolves upon resting. Other options represent either rarer conditions or complications seen later in the progression of the disease.
```

总结：

腹主动脉闭塞病最常见的临床表现是小腿肌群在运动中感到酸痛，在静息状态下可得到缓解；而选项中的其它情况要么较为罕见，要么仅出现在病情进展到晚期阶段。（注意：此处翻译为中文以符合要求） \n\n
